perturbation.original_text: An Assisted Listening Device ("ALD") is a Hearing Aids are amplification devices primarily designed to compensate for hearing loss, designed for listening only (through a microphone, telecoil, Direct Audio Input or other similar audio pathways and run on a low power hybrid circuit.
perturbation.changed_text: An Assisted Listening Device ("ALD") is defined as a device specifically designed for purposes other than compensating for hearing loss, focusing on clear communication in specific environments. Hearing Aids are amplification devices primarily designed to compensate for hearing loss, designed for listening only (through a microphone, telecoil, Direct Audio Input or other similar audio pathways and run on a low power hybrid circuit.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to the definition of "Assisted Listening Device" (ALD) introduces a contradiction. The original definition was somewhat overlapping with that of Hearing Aids, implying ALDs could also be amplification devices. The modified definition explicitly excludes ALDs from being devices that compensate for hearing loss, creating a conflict. This change contradicts the intended usage and applicability of the agreement, as the overlap or distinction between ALDs and Hearing Aids now becomes ambiguous and subject to interpretation, particularly affecting clauses related to exclusivity and royalty payments which depend on how products are classified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The purpose of this alliance is to exploit the parties' complimentary capabilities for producing DSP Technology products for the HH, ALD, and PADA markets.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Assisted Listening Device' (ALD) explicitly excludes devices that compensate for hearing loss. The contradicted text states that the alliance aims to exploit capabilities for producing products for the 'ALD' market, which is now ambiguous. If ALDs are not for hearing loss, but the alliance includes the HH market (presumably related to hearing), it creates a contradiction about the purpose and scope of the alliance, directly conflicting with the description of intended markets.

perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of 'Territory' to only include Victoria, Australia directly contradicts the clause that grants IntriCon a license to sell products 'throughout the Territory.' This creates uncertainty about the geographical scope of IntriCon's rights and obligations under the agreement. It creates an in-text contradiction, since the license grant explicitly says that it covers the entire world, whereas the definition of 'Territory' now only includes Victoria, Australia. This contradiction affects clauses related to exclusivity and royalty payments, as it is unclear whether these provisions now only apply to sales within Victoria.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dynamic Hearing grants to IntriCon in accordance with this Agreement, for the Term, a license, to Use Dynamic Hearing's Technology, Software and Documentation developed as of the Commencement Date to manufacture, import, sell and offer for sale throughout the Territory, Products containing Dynamic Hearing's Technology and Software. This grant specifically excludes products for Contact Centers, and the use of ADRO Technology in electrical stimulation of the auditory system.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Territory' as 'the world,' while the changed text narrows the definition to 'Victoria, Australia.' The contradicted text grants IntriCon a license to sell products 'throughout the Territory.' This creates a direct contradiction because the scope of the license is now drastically reduced from worldwide to only Victoria, Australia, rendering the original grant incompatible with the new definition. This contradiction introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding IntriCon's rights and obligations, directly breaking consistency.

perturbation.original_text: Improvements mean any modification, improvement, enhancement or development to the Licensed Subject Matter excluding always a development, modification, improvement or enhancement that is patentable in its own right or which is proprietary information of IntriCon.
perturbation.changed_text: Improvements mean any modification, improvement, enhancement or development to the Licensed Subject Matter including any development, modification, improvement or enhancement that is patentable in its own right or which is proprietary information of IntriCon.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "excluding always" to "including", the definition of Improvements now contradicts the implied ownership and rights associated with patentable inventions or proprietary information. The original definition clearly stated that if IntriCon were to create something patentable when improving on Licensed Subject Matter, Dynamic Hearing would not have a right to this IP. The revised definition indicates that Dynamic Hearing would now have a right to these patentable improvements. This creates uncertainty regarding IP ownership and licensing rights, particularly if IntriCon develops significant enhancements to the Licensed Subject Matter.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IntriCon accepts that Dynamic Hearing owns all rights in relation to the Licensed Subject Matter except for those rights being specifically granted hereunder, and that Dynamic Hearing is under no obligation to provide the source code of any software.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, by including patentable inventions or proprietary information of IntriCon in the definition of 'Improvements', directly contradicts the contradicted text. The contradicted text states that Dynamic Hearing owns all rights in the Licensed Subject Matter except those specifically granted. The original text explicitly excluded IntriCon's patentable inventions and proprietary information from the definition of 'Improvements'. Changing the definition to include such inventions creates a conflict: now Dynamic Hearing may have a right to improvements that IntriCon previously retained exclusive ownership of, resulting in an in-text contradiction.
